backend: custom MLX LoRA ensemble (K=4) probe-as-loss; N=120 test; per-seed PAIRED bootstrap CI on the disjoint audit-probe accuracy delta
verdict: STRONG anti-Goodhart evidence, NEAR-rigorous -> w5 stays OPEN. With N=120 + paired CIs, 2 of 3 seeds' audit-delta 95% CI EXCLUDES 0 (seed0 0.617->0.90 CI[+0.20,+0.375]; seed1 0.90->0.95 CI[+0.017,+0.092]); seed2 0.925->0.975 CI[-0.008,+0.108] grazes 0. ALL seeds: goodhartGap<=0.15 and from-scratch audit separates 0.958-0.967. High-base seeds (0.90,0.925) ALSO improved -> not pure regression-to-mean. This substantiates the K=4 ensemble as a genuine anti-Goodhart training method (v1 single-probe GAMED the audit; the ensemble IMPROVES it). Not a full close: seed2's CI grazes 0 (needs larger N / matched base), and rows stay OPEN per the no-overclaim contract.
